threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it 'the tube' instead of 'the underground' and keep looking for 'central' signs on the Overground. Classic rookie mistake!
Heard a guy on the N36 swear he saw a raccoon near the pub by the park. Sketchy, but it explains the bins being wrecked last weekend.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a hipster with a pet chicken casually crossing Karl-Marx-Straße. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club in an old power plant where the morning light streams through broken windows as ravers keep dancing with the sun.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they still call it 'Civic Center' instead of 'Twitter Park' and remember when the plaza had a fountain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo pull out a saxophone and play ‘Take Five’ on the 6. Muni’s unpredictability showcases our city’s quirky heart.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we're all about Gaudí, but the real vibe is in the local plazas where families chat and kids play – that’s our heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask locals for directions to Parc Güell—no one from here goes that route anymore.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 243 bus will get you anywhere near on time—news flash, mate: it’s more a suggestion than a schedule.
Heard a guy on the 333 arguing with the driver about how the bus was haunted. Claimed it only stops for ghosts at midnight.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a kid trading Pokémon cards with an old man outside the Späti, both of them arguing over which Charizard was cooler. That's real Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a café that doubles as a tattoo parlor. Coffee and ink, all in one spot—perfect for the indecisive!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local by how they know to avoid BART on Fridays; only tourists would brave the Transbay Tube with all that smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 bus at 5th and Market, a guy serenading the crowd with a ukulele while we waited. Classic Tenderloin moment—both chaotic and kind of beautiful.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's charm is all about the plazas, but they miss the real magic in the tiny bakeries tucked away on side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by the way they hesitantly order a café amb llet and still look around like they expect a barista to answer in English.
